items.title: Bitcoin dips to $59,700 as Iran de-escalation lifts stocks but not crypto
items.summary: U.S. equity futures rose after reports the U.S. and Iran agreed to halt strikes and resume talks. Bitcoin has barely moved, still down 6.8% on the week.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Excessive AI spending risks global financial consequences, BIS warns
items.summary: The AI investment surge is a potential flashpoint for systemic risk, “as financing has relied on enormous debt and highly leveraged nonbank structures that can rapidly unwind,” one analyst said in response to the report.

items.title: $4 billion gone. Spot bitcoin ETFs are on track for their worst month on record
items.summary: Investors have pulled out $4 billion from the U.S.-listed spot bitcoin ETFs in June, the highest outflow on record.

items.title: Dubai crypto market hits 50 licensed firms after new VARA approval
items.summary: Dubai’s VARA has licensed its 50th crypto firm, though only 39 VASPs were fully operational at the end of 2025 as the emirate’s regulated market continues to expand.

items.title: Pioneering zk-rollup Loopring closes DEX, citing lack of adoption
